title: Frequently Asked Questions
description: Common questions from Indians travelling to or living in Belgium.

items.question: Do Indian citizens need a visa to visit Belgium?
items.answer: Yes. Indian passport holders need a Schengen Type C visa for tourism or short business visits of up to 90 days within a 180-day period. Apply through VFS Global.

items.question: How long does a Belgium visa take to process?
items.answer: Typically around 15 calendar days for a complete application, but it can take up to 30-60 days during busy periods, so apply as early as possible (up to 6 months ahead, and no later than 15 days before travel).

items.question: What is the emergency number in Belgium?
items.answer: Dial 112 for any emergency (ambulance, fire or police) anywhere in Belgium — it's toll-free. You can also dial 101 directly for police or 100 for ambulance/fire.

items.question: Where is the Indian Embassy in Belgium located?
items.answer: The Embassy of India is at 217 Chaussée de Vleurgat, 1050 Brussels. It covers Belgium, Luxembourg and the EU institutions.

items.question: How do I get from Brussels Airport into the city?
items.answer: The Brussels Airport Express train takes about 18 minutes to Brussels-Central (~€8.90). The STIB Airport Bus (Line 12) is a cheaper alternative into the EU Quarter (~€7.90 with a MOBIB or contactless card).

items.question: What currency is used in Belgium?
items.answer: The Euro (EUR). Contactless card payments are widely accepted, and ATMs are common in all cities.

items.question: Is Belgium safe for solo travellers?
items.answer: Belgium is generally very safe. As in any European city, be alert for pickpocketing in crowded tourist areas and on public transport.

items.question: Can I find vegetarian and Jain food in Belgium?
items.answer: Yes, especially in Brussels and Antwerp (particularly around the Diamond District), which have dedicated Indian vegetarian and Jain-friendly restaurants.

items.question: What languages are spoken in Belgium?
items.answer: Belgium has three official languages: Dutch (Flanders), French (Wallonia) and German (small eastern region). Brussels is officially bilingual French/Dutch. English is widely understood in cities.

items.question: Do I need to register with the local municipality after arriving?
items.answer: Yes, if staying longer than 90 days, non-EU nationals must register their address at the local commune/gemeente (municipality) within 8 working days of arrival.

items.question: How do public transport tickets work across Belgium?
items.answer: Each region has its own operator — STIB (Brussels), De Lijn (Flanders), TEC (Wallonia) and SNCB (national trains). MOBIB cards and contactless bank cards work on most local networks; SNCB tickets are separate.

items.question: What should I pack for Belgium's weather?
items.answer: Belgium has a mild, rainy maritime climate. Pack layers, a waterproof jacket, and comfortable walking shoes year-round; winters are cold and grey, summers are mild.

items.question: Can I use my Indian driving licence in Belgium?
items.answer: An Indian driving licence with an International Driving Permit (IDP) can be used for short stays (up to 185 days). For residents, conversion to a Belgian licence is required within that window.

items.question: Are there Hindu temples or gurudwaras in Belgium?
items.answer: Yes — Brussels and Antwerp have Hindu temples and gurudwaras serving the Indian community. See the Indian Community section for details.

items.question: How do I renew my Indian passport while in Belgium?
items.answer: Passport renewal is handled by the Embassy of India in Brussels. Check indianembassybrussels.gov.in for the latest application forms and appointment system.

items.question: Is healthcare good in Belgium?
items.answer: Belgium has a high-quality healthcare system. Visitors should carry travel insurance; residents must enrol in mandatory health insurance (mutualité/ziekenfonds).

items.question: Can I work in Belgium on a tourist visa?
items.answer: No. A Schengen tourist visa does not permit employment. You need a work permit or single permit tied to a specific job offer.

items.question: What is the best way to travel between Belgian cities?
items.answer: SNCB trains are fast, frequent and connect all major cities — usually the best option over driving, especially between Brussels, Antwerp, Ghent and Bruges.

items.question: Do I need an adapter for electrical outlets in Belgium?
items.answer: Belgium uses the Type E plug (230V, 50Hz), the same as most of continental Europe. Indian plugs (Type C/D) will need an adapter.

items.question: How do I find English-speaking doctors in Belgium?
items.answer: Many doctors in Brussels and other major cities speak English, especially in international clinics. See the Healthcare section for guidance on finding one.
